title: Meoschium rugosum
intro: Meoschium rugosum, also known as hairy bugloss, is a flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as skin conditions and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Meoschium rugosum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Meoschium rugosum is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, which is used to treat a range of conditions, including coughs, colds, and fever.
taxonomy: Meoschium rugosum is classified within the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the order Lamiales. This plant is further divided into the genus Meoschium and the species rugosum. Within the family Boraginaceae, Meoschium rugosum is closely related to other plants such as Lithospermum and Mertensia. The classification of Meoschium rugosum is based on its morphology and genetic characteristics.
